name: HNMS Jan van Gelder (J 60)
events: 11 May 1940 HrMs O 13 (Lt.Cdr. E.H. Vorster, RNN) arrived at the Downs escorted by minesweeper HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (1)

12 May 1940 Around 0800 A/12, the unfinished light cruiser HrMs Jacob van Heemskerck (Lt.Cdr. A. van Foreest, RNN), minesweeper HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN), submarine HrMs O 13 (Lt.Cdr. E.H. Vorster, RNN) and the unfinished submarines HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) and HrMs O 22 (Lt.Cdr. A.M. Valkenburg, RNN) departed the Downs for Portsmouth where they arrived around 2000 A/12. (1)
26 Mar 1942 At 0910 A/26, near Trevose Head, HMS Thunderbolt (Lt.Cdr. C.B. Crouch, DSO, RN), made rendezvous with her escort towards Holy Loch, HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (2)

1 Apr 1942 Around 1045 A/1, P 44 (Lt. T.E. Barlow, RN) and P 615 (Lt.Cdr. W.S. Hall, RN) departed their builders yard at Barrow for Holy Loch. They were escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (3)
8 Apr 1942 At 1700 B/8, HMS P 46 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN) departed her builders yard at Barrow for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (4)
12 Apr 1942 Having completed her refit at the Chatham Dockyard, HMS Ursula (Lt. R.B. Lakin, DSC, RN) departed Sheerness for Holy Loch via Spithead.
She made the passage to Portsmouth in convoy CW 75.
[For more info on this convoy see the event 'Convoy CW 75' for 12 April 1942.]

On arrival in Spithead HMS Ursula was to proceed to Solent Western Gate for onward escort to end of QZS 284. Thence diving by day through position 'A' (180° - Start Point - 20 miles) and position 'B' (160° - Lizard Head - 20 miles to make rendezvous in position 180° - Lizard Head at 0700 B/15 for onward escort to Holy Loch as would be arranged by Capt. S.3.

In a signal timed 1005 B/12, the Capt. S.3 stated that it was his intention to sail HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) at 1900 B/13 to make rendezvous with HMS Ursula in position 180° - Lizard Head - 5 miles at 0700 B/15 and escort her to Holy Loch. (5)

17 Apr 1942 HMS Ursula (Lt. R.B. Lakin, DSC, RN) arrived at Holy Loch for a period of trials and training. She had been escorted north through the Irish Sea by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN).
[No log for April 1942 is available so details for the training exercises during this month can not be given.] (5)
19 Apr 1942 HNoMS Uredd (Lt. Røren) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (4)
22 Apr 1942 ORP Jastrzab (Lt.Cdr. B. Romanowski) departed Holy Loch for Lerwick. She is escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (6)
25 Apr 1942 Around 1145 A/25, HMS Trident (Lt. A.R. Hezlet, DSC, RN) ended her 27th war patrol at Lerwick.
She departed for Holy Loch around 1500 A/25, escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN).

3 May 1942 Around 0800 B/3, HMS Utmost (Lt. A.W. Langridge, RN). departed Blyth for passage to Holy Loch. She made the passage together with HMS Thunderbolt (Lt.Cdr. C.B. Crouch, DSO and Bar, RN). They were escorted by HMS Scalby Wyke (Skr. C.A. Grimmer, RNR).
Around 1845 B/3, off Dundee, they were joined by the Dutch submarine HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) that was on passage to Scapa Flow.
Around 1300 B/3, HrMs O 14 and HMS Scalby Wyke proceeded to Scapa Flow while HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) took over the escort of HMS Utmost and HMS Thunderbolt. (7)
10 May 1942 P 212 (Lt. J.H. Bromage, DSC, RN) departed her builders yard for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (8)
11 May 1942 HMS Utmost (Lt. A.W. Langridge, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area. These included a practice attack on HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (7)

13 May 1942 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) departed Rothesay for Lerwick. She made the passage together with HMS P 46 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN) and HMS P 614 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, RN). They were escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (9)
21 May 1942 HMS Utmost (Lt. A.W. Langridge, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (7)
22 May 1942 HrMs O 9 (Lt. J.F. Drijfhout Van Hooff, RNN) conducted attack exercises on the Clyde together with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (10)

22 May 1942 HMS P 45 (Lt. H.B. Turner, RN) is undocked at Kames Bay. She then conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area on a target that was towed by HMS Z 5 (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). These were followed by practice attacks during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) served as target and finally night exercises with special forces. (11)
31 May 1942 HMS H 34 (Lt. F.D.G. Challis, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for Rothesay. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (12)

12 Jun 1942 At 1745 B/12, HMS P 46 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN) and HMS P 222 (Lt. A.J. MacKenzie, RN) both departed Holy Loch for their passage to Gibraltar. Both submarines were to join the Mediterranean Fleet. They were escorted until the vicinity Bishops Rock (270° - Bishop Rock - 3 miles) by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN).
At 2240 B/12, HrMs Jan van Gelder parted company with the submarines.
P 46 was routed through the following positions; Position 'E' (49°59'N, 07°01'E), Position 'P' (49°31'N, 13°29'E) to Position 'G' (43°01'N, 13°29'E), E.T.A. 0300 B/22.
From position 'G' she was to proceed Gibraltar as will be ordered by the Flag Officer Commanding North Atlantic (F.O.C.N.A.). (13)
24 Jun 1942 HMS P 212 (Lt. J.H. Bromage, DSC, RN) departed Holy Loch for Lerwick. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (14)

12 Jul 1942 ORP Sokol (Kpt.mar. (Lt.Cdr.) B. Karnicki, DSO) departed Holy Loch for Blyth via a stopover at Lerwick on the 14th. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) all the way. (4)
16 Jul 1942 Around 1400 B/16, HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) departed from Dundee for Holy Loch. She is escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN).
Around 0630 B/17, they anchored in Widewall Bay, Orkney Islands due to bad weather conditions. They entered Scapa Flow around 1400 B/17.
Around 1100 B/18, they departed Scapa Flow to continue their passage to the Clyde. (15)

18 Jul 1942 Around 1800 B/18, HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) and her escort, HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN), made rendezvous with HMS P 212 (Lt. J.H. Bromage, DSC, RN) and her current escort HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) coming from Lerwick. HMS P 212 then joined while HMS La Capricieuse parted company to return to Lerwick. (16)

1 Aug 1942 HMS Sturgeon (Lt.Cdr. M.R.G. Wingfield, RN) departed Holy Loch for Lerwick. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). On 3 August HMS Stella Pegasi (T/Lt. F.S. Howell, RNR) took over the escort. (17)
4 Aug 1942 HMS P 511 (Lt. D.E.O. Watson, DSC, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Rothesay. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (18)
6 Aug 1942 HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) conducted RDF trials in the Clyde area during which she was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (15)

7 Aug 1942 HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) serving as the target. Also a deep dive trial was carried out. (15)
12 Aug 1942 Around 0300 B/12, HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) departed from Holy Loch for Gibraltar. This is the first leg of the trip to the Far East where she is to join the Eastern Fleet.

During the passage south through the Irish Sea she was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). HMS P 219 (Lt. N.L.A. Jewell, RN) and HMS P 48 (Lt. M.E. Faber, RN) were also in company.
Around 2120 B/13, near the Scilly Islands, the submarines parted company with their escort. (19)
2 Sep 1942 HMS P 311 (Cdr. R.D. Cayley, DSO and 2 Bars, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) served as target. (20)

13 Sep 1942 HMS P 311 (Cdr. R.D. Cayley, DSO and 2 Bars, RN) departed from Holy Loch for Plymouth. She has to be modified at the Devonport Dockyard to carry the containers that hold the Chariot human torpedoes. During the passage south through the Irish Sea she was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (20)
16 Sep 1942 HMS H 50 (Lt. G.E. Hunt, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for Rothesay. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (21)

27 Sep 1942 HMS P 614 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, RN) departed Lerwick for Holy Loch. She made the passage together with HMS P 615 (Lt. P.E. Newstead, RN). They were initially escorted by HMS Preston North End (Lt. K.A. Vasey, MBE, RNR) until HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) took over on the 28th. (22)

7 Oct 1942 Around 0915 A/7, Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) departed her builders yard at Barrow for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (23)
8 Oct 1942 Around 0445 A/8, Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) and HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) arrived at Holy Loch from Barrow in Furness.

Dolfijn was placed in full commission (taken over from the shipyard) at Holy Loch later in the morning on completion of the acceptance trials. She was then to start a period of trials and training in Clyde area. (24)

26 Oct 1942 HMS Otway (Cdr. H.F. Bone, DSO and Bar, DSC and Bar, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area for the C.O.Q.C. (Commanding Officers Qualifying Course) with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (25)
26 Oct 1942 HMS P 312 (Lt. R.M. Favell, RN) conducted special trials in the Clyde area with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (26)

1 Nov 1942 Shrotly before 1700 A/1, P 339 (Lt.Cdr. M.R.G. Wingfield, RN) departed from her builders yard at Barrow for Holy Loch. P 49 (Lt. J.P. Fyfe, RN) was in company. They were escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (27)
3 Nov 1942 HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) conducted radar trials with aircraft and HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (28)

10 Nov 1942 HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A. E. Johnston, RN) and HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) acted as targets. (28)

22 Nov 1942 HMS P 223 (Lt. G.D.N. Milner, DSC, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) served as target. On completion of these exercises noise trials were carried out in Loch Goil. (29)

25 Nov 1942 HMS P 339 (Lt.Cdr. M.R.G. Wingfield, DSO, RN) and HMS P 49 (Lt. J.P. Fyfe, RN) both conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) served as target. (30)

26 Nov 1942 During 26/27 November 1942, HMS P 339 (Lt.Cdr. M.R.G. Wingfield, DSO, RN) and HMS P 49 (Lt. J.P. Fyfe, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) and HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN) served as targets. These included night exercises. (30)
3 Feb 1943 HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area with a target towed by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (31)
4 Feb 1943 HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. J.W. McCoy, DSC, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area with a target towed by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (31)
5 Feb 1943 HMS P 223 (Lt. G.D.N. Milner, DSC, RN) conducted gunnery exercises in the Clyde area on a target that was being towed by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (32)
11 Feb 1943 Around 1600 A/11, P 316 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, DSO, RN) departed her builders yard at Barrow for Holy Loch. She is escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (33)
12 Feb 1943 Around 1330 A/12, P 316 (Lt. D.J. Beckley, DSO, RN) and HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) arrived at Holy Loch from Barrow. (34)
18 Feb 1943 Stubborn (Lt. A.A. Duff, RN) departed her builders yard at Liverpool for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (35)

25 Feb 1943 HMS Usurper (Lt. D.R.O. Mott, DSC, RN) was conducting exercises on Inmarnock Water when she was ordered to search in the Clyde area for her missing sister ship HMS Vandal (Lt. J.S. Bridger, RN).

The following submarines / ships were also ordered to search for HMS Vandal (which was not found until 1994); HMS Graph (Lt. P.B. Marriott, DSO, RN), HMS L 27 (Lt. W.N. Eade, RNR), HMS Severn (Lt.Cdr. A.N.G. Campbell, RN), HMS Stubborn (Lt. A.A. Duff, RN), HMS Whitehall (Cdr. C.L.de H. Bell, RD, RNR), HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN), HMS Kingfisher (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) G.H. Gandy, RN), HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR), HMS Cygnet (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) F.B. Proudfoot, RN), HMS Tedworth (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) G.H. Warren, RN) and HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (36)
6 Mar 1943 HMS Satyr (Lt. J.P.H. Oakley, DSC, RN) conducted attack exercises in the Clyde area during which HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN) served as target. (37)
12 Mar 1943 HMS Stubborn (Lt. A.A. Duff, RN) conducted RDF (radar) exercises in the Clyde area with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (38)
16 Mar 1943 Surf (Lt. D. Lambert, DSC, RN) departed her builders yard for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (39)
19 Mar 1943 HMS Stubborn (Lt. A.A. Duff, RN) conducted RDF (radar) exercises in the Clyde area with HrMs Jan van Gelder (Lt.Cdr. P.L.M. van Geen, RNN). (38)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
